title: Battle for Budapest
pages_or_sections: Preface, preview PDF p. 11 (lines 228-250 in web parse)
strengths: Scholarly book dedicated to the battle; gives explicit trapped civilian denominator and headline death totals for civilians, defenders, and attackers.
limitations: Preview exposes only part of the text; the preface gives aggregate totals but not the full methodological appendix behind them.
notes: Core high-quality source for Budapest headline numbers.

title: Siege of Budapest
author_or_org: Wikipedia summary page citing Ungváry and related works
strengths: Quick-access page with battle table and explicit civilian mortality decomposition linked to cited sources.
limitations: Secondary summary rather than the underlying primary or scholarly text; some table rows mix casualties and deaths; should be used critically.
notes: Used mainly to expose battle-table strengths and the civilian decomposition into military action versus starvation/disease/other.

title: The Holocaust in Hungary
author_or_org: Wikipedia summary page citing Braham and related Holocaust works
strengths: Provides an explicit 10,000-15,000 range for Arrow Cross shootings in Budapest during the relevant months.
limitations: The date window is broader than the strict siege episode and the page is secondary rather than the underlying Holocaust monograph.
notes: Used to set a conservative lower/upper bound for Arrow Cross execution deaths relevant to the Budapest episode.

title: Siege of Breslau
strengths: Gives city-remnant civilian denominator, garrison strength, and illustrates the huge spread in civilian death estimates.
limitations: Tertiary synthesis; explicit civilian death numbers conflict sharply and likely mix evacuation losses, city deaths, and later mortality.
notes: Useful for episode screening and for explaining why no full Breslau DR estimate is completed here.

title: European Migrations and Their Governments in the Context of WWII
author_or_org: Texas A&M repository thesis (author not captured in snippet)
strengths: Quotes Hargreaves with separate figures for deaths during flight and deaths while the city was under attack.
limitations: Secondhand quotation rather than the cited monograph itself; not enough detail to cleanly separate direct violent from indirect mortality.
notes: Useful only as a screening clue; not strong enough by itself for core DR numerators.

title: So fiel Königsberg
date_scope: April 1945 with retrospective discussion of siege conditions
strengths: Provides explicit population and garrison estimates for the final assault and directly states that bloody losses remain unclear.
limitations: Memoir by fortress commander; not a neutral casualty accounting and not a modern scholarly audit.
notes: Useful for denominator reconstruction and for documenting the weakness of military-death numerators.

title: The Evacuation of East Prussia (chapter in Violence in Defeat)
strengths: Recent scholarship; directly links the halt in evacuation to roughly 100,000 civilians in Königsberg and says civilian deaths reached the tens of thousands.
limitations: Summary page gives qualitative rather than audited numeric casualty series; not enough for a clean direct-death numerator.
notes: Strong denominator/context source for why the city merits its own episode.

title: The siege of Festung Königsberg - Documents
strengths: Explicitly flags Soviet pamphlet numbers as inflated/fictitious and gives a more realistic garrison strength.
limitations: Not itself a full casualty audit; primarily a source-critical note about pamphlets.
notes: Key reason not to ingest the famous 42,000-dead claim as a direct death series.

title: Belgrade offensive
author_or_org: Wikipedia summary page citing campaign histories
pages_or_sections: On the streets of Belgrade; infobox
geographic_scope: Belgrade and wider Serbian offensive theater
strengths: Provides a clear date window for the street-battle phase and distinguishes some assault-on-Belgrade-only partisan losses from wider operational casualties.
limitations: Does not provide a clean city-civilian direct-death numerator or a city-only civilian denominator; wider offensive and earlier bombings contaminate the picture.
notes: Enough to keep Belgrade on the shortlist, not enough for a clean DR estimate in this pass.
